source_reviews.notes: The official Census NAICS page and code-specific query resolve, but the reopened text rendering did not expose the 541910 result text. The scope claim is nonnumeric and consistent with the selected official code, but direct page-level verification was incomplete.

The URL currently resolves to the 2024 GRIT Business & Innovation Report, whose public page says GenAI adopters replace entire work processes and highlights caution around AI at work. It does not expose the packet's exact 2025 title or its precise permanent-shift, human-centered-insights and good-enough-trust wording.

The 2025 ICC/ESOMAR code defines an individual/person as a human being distinct from a synthetic persona, defines primary data as collected directly from or about an individual for research, and requires transparency and human oversight around AI and synthetic data.

Kantar confirms completion of its acquisition of the tech-driven consumer and market-intelligence company Numerator and states that terms were not released. The release is dated July 6, 2021, making it historical rather than current-cadence evidence.
NIQ's April 7, 2025 release confirms its agreement to acquire Gastrograph AI and explicitly describes faster speed to market and lower costs from the AI-enabled sensory-insights platform.
NIQ's August 6, 2025 release says Mtrix was the latest of nine tuck-in acquisitions. The source is narrative context and is not referenced by a finalized score input.

WPP's April 3, 2025 release confirms acquisition of InfoSum and describes privacy-safe data collaboration, marketing intelligence and AI-enabled infrastructure. The packet correctly treats it as a large adjacent strategic transaction rather than a 541910 boutique comp.

The report presents Q1 2026 marketing/digital-agency EV/adjusted-EBITDA ranges of 3.0x-4.5x for $1 million-$3 million EBITDA and 4.0x-6.5x for $3 million-$10 million. The packet discloses that this is an adjacent proxy rather than a 541910 transaction series.

findings.explanation: The exact Census URL resolves, but its dynamically rendered code-specific definition was not visible in the reopened text extraction, preventing complete direct verification from that page rendering.
findings.materiality: This is a nonnumeric official-scope claim used only as background for an estimate of pricing structure; it does not contradict the code identity or support a score-driving numeric selection, so it is not shown to move a factor, score or thesis.
findings.remediation: Preserve the official code URL and, on a future run, add a stable Census manual or downloadable definition page that exposes the 541910 text directly.
findings.publication_caveat: Validator: The official Census 541910 URL resolved, but its code-specific definition was not exposed in the reopened text rendering, so direct page-level verification of that scope claim was incomplete.

findings.explanation: Source S2 currently resolves to the 2024 GRIT Business & Innovation Report, not the packet's stated 2025 report. Its public page supports GenAI replacing work processes and industry caution, but not the packet's exact permanent-shift, human-centered-insights or good-enough-trust characterizations.
findings.materiality: The core direction of workflow automation is supported, and every affected numeric input is explicitly an estimate with low or medium confidence, broad ranges and caveats; no contradictory adoption percentage or score-driving numeric fact is asserted. The mismatch therefore does not demonstrate a factor reversal or thesis-changing error.
findings.remediation: Correct the report year/title and cite a stable page or report passage for any exact trust and human-centered characterizations in a future run.
findings.publication_caveat: Validator: Source S2 resolves to the 2024 GRIT Business & Innovation Report, not the stated 2025 report; its public page supports GenAI replacing work processes but not all of the packet's exact qualitative characterizations.

findings.explanation: The Kantar-Numerator acquisition source is dated July 2021. It proves historical participation by Kantar but is weak evidence that Kantar is an active consolidator in the 2025-2026 target market.
findings.materiality: The other two counted buyers have 2025 transactions, the packet already discloses that all cited buyers and targets are large or adjacent, and reducing the active-consolidator count would increase rather than reduce deterministic B under the campaign formula while leaving the kill verdict intact. The low-confidence exit multiple is independently bounded by the disclosed adjacent S8 range.
findings.remediation: Use a recent Kantar transaction or lower-middle-market 541910 buyer record to support current activity in a future run.
findings.publication_caveat: Validator: Kantar's Numerator acquisition is from July 2021, so it shows historical buyer participation rather than current 2025-2026 consolidation cadence.

publication_caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: 4-digit 5419 is materially contaminated by veterinary services and is not a usable representation of 541910 staffing.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The high residual share is a modeling consequence of the bad proxy, not an observed occupation distribution.
inputs.pi_dist: Sources support AI/data competitive change, not a measured 541910 pass-through rate.
inputs.pi_moat: No source in this packet measures lower-middle-market client retention or founder dependency.
inputs.t50_years: No public full-industry effective-adoption percentage was reopened.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Not an observed NAICS adoption statistic; it intentionally remains below 50%, requiring positive t50.
inputs.historical_analogs: No causal historical study is claimed.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Unobserved estimate; not a demographic claim.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Absence of evidence here is not evidence of absence.
inputs.active_consolidators: These buyers and targets are global/data/technology adjacent and are not direct evidence of small US 541910 acquisition appetite.
inputs.buy_mult: No disclosed lower-middle-market 541910 transaction multiple was reopened; the cited marketing-agency category is a proxy.
inputs.exit_mult: Strategic announcements disclose no transaction multiples and are materially larger/adjacent to the intended target set.
inputs.pricing_structure: No reopened source quantifies the recurring-revenue share for this NAICS code.

Validator: The official Census 541910 URL resolved, but its code-specific definition was not exposed in the reopened text rendering, so direct page-level verification of that scope claim was incomplete.

Validator: Source S2 resolves to the 2024 GRIT Business & Innovation Report, not the stated 2025 report; its public page supports GenAI replacing work processes but not all of the packet's exact qualitative characterizations.
Validator: Kantar's Numerator acquisition is from July 2021, so it shows historical buyer participation rather than current 2025-2026 consolidation cadence.
summary: Mechanics, exact artifact identity, fresh finalization, memo rendering and arithmetic all pass. All eight registered sources were reopened. The low-confidence kill thesis remains directionally useful and avoids terminal/capture double counting, but publication must retain 13 authored caveats plus three validator caveats for incomplete Census page rendering, the GRIT year/content mismatch and the age of the Kantar acquisition evidence. No finding is shown to reverse a factor or materially change the verdict.
